findings.summary: Current MCP dialog already has 261 lines of enhancement work (add remote server, health indicators, toggle connect/disconnect, dropdown delete) but only pretends to delete (just disconnects), only supports remote servers, has no edit capability
NO MCP-specific ElectronAPI methods exist — the desktop app uses SDK client HTTP calls to the sidecar server, not Electron IPC
MCP config schema has two discriminated types: Local (command, environment, enabled, timeout) and Remote (url, enabled, headers, oauth, timeout) with discriminator 'type'
MCP.Service add() stores config in-memory only (s.config[name] = mcp) — not persisted to config file. No remove() or update() methods exist on the interface
Management dialog pattern: List with search/group/sort, create via dialog-edit, inline edit/delete buttons, confirm-before-delete pattern, persistence via electron-store IPC
HTTP API exposes 6 endpoint groups: GET status, POST add, POST authStart/Callback/Authenticate, DELETE authRemove, POST connect/disconnect — the SDK client generates from this OpenAPI spec
